items.t: Baltic Dry Index Edges Down on Tuesday
items.x: The Baltic dry index decreased 1 point to 2,670 on Tuesday, following a 2.9% fall on Monday. The panamax index, which usually carry 60,000 to 70,000 tons of coal or grain, dropped 2.1% to 2,181. Also, the supramax index...

items.t: War risk insurance under pressure as volatility reshapes shipping routes
items.x: From the Red Sea to the Strait of Hormuz, overlapping conflicts have disrupted major trade routes and challenged the assumptions on which war risk coverage has long depended. This escalation of geopolitical tensions...

items.t: Tanker attacks at CPC disrupt oil loading for Kazakhstan main export route
items.x: Caspian Pipeline Consortium oil loading was disrupted again July 20, after a fourth tanker loading at the network was attacked in as many days, CPC said in a statement. Ukrainian attacks on CPC have intensified...

items.t: Capital Ship Management, K Shipbuilding and LR target scalable wind-assisted propulsion for MR tanker sector

items.t: Hormuz on a Knife-Edge: Shipping Pays the Price as the Iran-U.S. Ceasefire Unravels
items.x: The Strait of Hormuz is bleeding trade confidence again. Barely a month after Washington and Tehran signed a memorandum of understanding meant to end months of tanker attacks, the truce has come apart. Both sides are...

items.t: Two Tankers Carrying Saudi Crude Make U-turns in Red Sea After Houthi Warning
items.x: Two oil tankers loaded with Saudi crude for China and India made U-turns in the Red Sea on Tuesday, heading towards the Suez Canal rather than braving the Yemeni coast at the sea's mouth following a warning from Yemen's...

items.t: Global Trade Faces Fresh Test as Panama Canal Curbs Bookings
items.x: Global trade faces a fresh headache on top of the months-long snarls in the Middle East that have roiled flows, with the Panama Canal moving to curtail some vessel-booking slots because of water-supply challenges.

items.t: Tankers turn back after Houthi threat to Saudi shipping
items.x: Two tankers carrying Saudi crude and a third due to load at Yanbu have reversed course following Houthi threats against ships calling at the kingdom’s ports. The COSCO Shipping-managed VLCC Xin Long Yang, carrying...

items.t: Second Tanker Abandoned in 24 Hours After Latest Strait of Hormuz Attack
items.x: A second commercial tanker has been abandoned by its crew in less than 24 hours after being struck by an unidentified projectile near the Strait of Hormuz, according to the...

items.t: Houthis Warn All Ships Against Calling at Saudi Arabia’s Ports
items.x: A message from Yemen’s Houthi rebels to shipowners warned all vessels against calling at Saudi Arabian ports, shedding light on the scope of a threat that puts millions of barrels of exports from the kingdom at risk.
